axioms.name: Operational cost and restoration cost must be separated
axioms.statement: The cost of continuing along a path is not the same as the cost of abandoning, revising, or reopening that path.

axioms.name: Exception compression lowers operational cost
axioms.statement: When exception frequency falls, routine continuation becomes cheaper.

axioms.name: Cheap continuation increases repetition
axioms.statement: A path with lower operational cost is selected more often.

axioms.name: Fixation raises restoration cost
axioms.statement: As path fixation increases, the cost of restoration, revision, or reopening rises.

axioms.name: Low-cost convergence generates practical irreversibility
axioms.statement: Repeated low-cost operation can manufacture the restoration cost explosion by which irreversibility is later judged.
